name: Gjirokastër
description: A UNESCO-listed Ottoman fortress city draped over a mountain ridge, with stone towers, cobblestone lanes, and a museum-city atmosphere largely untouched by mass tourism.
image_hint: stone fortress city on mountain ridge

name: Matera
description: Ancient cave dwellings carved into a ravine — Sassi di Matera is one of the oldest continuously inhabited settlements on Earth, now a luminous backdrop for candlelit dinners.
image_hint: ancient cave dwellings at sunset

name: Hsipaw
description: A sleepy Shan State hill town where local tea plantations, Buddhist monasteries, and ethnic minority villages reveal a Myanmar most tourists never see.
image_hint: misty mountain tea plantation

name: Huacachina
description: A dreamlike oasis village rising from the heart of massive sand dunes — the last natural desert oasis in the Americas, where dune buggies and stargazing define the hours.
image_hint: oasis lagoon surrounded by sand dunes

name: Chefchaouen
description: The Blue Pearl of Morocco — every alley, wall, and staircase painted in celestial shades of blue, nestled in the Rif Mountains with a medina that feels like wandering inside a painting.
image_hint: blue painted alleyway in mountain village

name: Kotor
description: A perfectly preserved medieval walled city pressed between a fjord-like bay and vertiginous limestone cliffs — Adriatic perfection with a fraction of Dubrovnik's crowds.
image_hint: medieval city walls with bay backdrop

name: Luang Prabang
description: A UNESCO World Heritage town at the confluence of the Mekong and Nam Khan rivers, where saffron-robed monks collect alms at dawn and French colonial villas host river-view cafes.
image_hint: monks in saffron robes at dawn by river

name: Socotra
description: The Galápagos of the Indian Ocean — an alien landscape of Dragon Blood trees, white sand beaches, and turquoise lagoons so otherworldly it feels like a different planet.
image_hint: dragon blood trees on alien landscape

name: Colmar
description: A candy-coloured Alsatian town of half-timbered houses reflected in flower-draped canals — the model for Beauty and the Beast's village, most magical during Christmas markets.
image_hint: colourful half-timbered houses along canal

name: Bagan
description: Over 2,000 ancient Buddhist temples and pagodas scattered across a vast archaeological plain — most stunning at sunrise when hot air balloons drift over the misty temple fields.
image_hint: ancient temples at sunrise with hot air balloons

name: Zanzibar Stone Town
description: A UNESCO-listed labyrinth of carved wooden doors, Arab-influenced architecture, and spice-scented alleyways on an island that was once the heart of the Indian Ocean spice trade.
image_hint: ornate carved wooden door in stone alley

name: Tbilisi Old Town
description: Sulfuric bathhouses, Persian-era bridges, and crumbling baroque balconies overlooking the Mtkvari River — a city at the crossroads of Europe and Asia with legendary wine and hospitality.
image_hint: colourful balconies overhanging river gorge

name: Hallstatt
description: A fairy-tale lakeside village so impossibly picturesque it inspired Disney's Arendelle — with salt mines dating back 7,000 years and mirror-calm Alpine reflections.
image_hint: lakeside alpine village with mountain reflection

name: Borobudur
description: The world's largest Buddhist temple, a 9th-century mandala of 2,672 relief panels and 504 Buddha statues rising from jungle mist — most transcendent at pre-dawn candlelight ceremony.
image_hint: ancient Buddhist temple rising from jungle mist at sunrise

name: Valparaíso
description: A UNESCO-listed port city of 42 hills, where every funicular ride reveals a new explosion of street art, bohemian cafes, and panoramic Pacific views — Chile's creative soul.
image_hint: colorful houses on hillside with street art

name: Faroe Islands
description: Eighteen volcanic islands between Norway and Iceland shrouded in perpetual mist, where dramatic sea cliffs drop into the North Atlantic and turf-roofed villages defy time.
image_hint: turf-roofed village on dramatic sea cliffs

name: Hoi An Ancient Town
description: A lantern-lit UNESCO trading port frozen in the 15th century, where tailor shops, Thu Bon river boat rides, and white rose dumplings create an unforgettable sensory world.
image_hint: lantern-lit river town at dusk

name: Cappadocia
description: Volcanic tuff sculpted by millennia of erosion into fairy chimneys and cave churches, where underground cities run 18 stories deep and dawn hot-air balloon rides feel divine.
image_hint: hot air balloons over fairy chimney rock formations at dawn

name: Plovdiv Old Town
description: Europe's oldest continuously inhabited city, its Old Town a hilltop maze of National Revival architecture, Roman amphitheatres, and an arts scene that punches far above its weight.
image_hint: colorful National Revival houses on hilltop

name: Oaxaca
description: Mexico's cultural capital — Zapotec pyramids, vibrant Day of the Dead celebrations, mescal distilleries, and a culinary scene crowned by seven varieties of mole sauce.
image_hint: colorful market with traditional textiles and marigolds
